sentence_reviews.text: Supervised fine-tuning and instruction tuning train models on curated input–response examples so they follow task descriptions and conversational conventions.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Ouyang arXiv abstract lines 37-40 describes aligning language models by fine-tuning with human feedback, collecting labeler demonstrations of desired behavior, and fine-tuning GPT-3 using supervised learning.
sentence_reviews.notes: The exact instruction-following SFT pipeline is directly supported by the inspected RLHF/InstructGPT source.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Supervised fine-tuning & instruction tuning is to connect Supervised learning, Large-scale pretraining to downstream capabilities including Parameter-efficient tuning, adapters & LoRA, Reward-model training, RLHF & RLAIF, Direct and online preference optimization.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Ouyang lines 37-40 supports supervised fine-tuning of pretrained GPT-3 before RLHF; LoRA lines 42-44 supports adaptation of pretrained models for downstream tasks; DPO lines 42-45 supports fine-tuning LMs to align with preferences.
sentence_reviews.notes: The upstream supervised/pretraining and RLHF/DPO/LoRA links are supported; RLAIF is not directly covered.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Parameter-efficient tuning, adapters & LoRA
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: LoRA arXiv abstract lines 42-44 describes large-scale pretraining and downstream adaptation, freezing pretrained weights and injecting trainable low-rank matrices for downstream tasks.
sentence_reviews.notes: LoRA is a parameter-efficient adaptation method; it need not always be instruction tuning specifically, so substrate wording is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Reward-model training, RLHF & RLAIF
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Ouyang arXiv abstract lines 38-40 describes supervised fine-tuning from demonstrations followed by rankings and RLHF on the supervised model.
sentence_reviews.notes: Directly supported for RLHF; RLAIF is not directly named, but the node label includes RLHF.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Direct and online preference optimization
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: DPO arXiv abstract lines 42-45 describes collecting relative-quality labels and fine-tuning LMs to align with preferences; Ouyang lines 38-40 describes supervised model further fine-tuned by RLHF.
sentence_reviews.notes: SFT as a starting point for preference optimization is supported, though online preference optimization is broader than DPO.

sentence_reviews.text: Results are sensitive to data quality, hyperparameters, compute budget, random variation, and evaluation contamination. Its direct dependencies include Supervised learning, Large-scale pretraining.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Ouyang lines 38-40 supports supervised learning on demonstrations from GPT-3 and human evaluation; the generic sensitivity and contamination clauses are not directly stated in the inspected abstract.
sentence_reviews.notes: Dependencies are supported; the broad limitations sentence should be narrowed or separately sourced.

sentence_reviews.text: Supervised fine-tuning & instruction tuning uses Supervised learning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Ouyang arXiv abstract line 38 says labeler demonstrations are used to fine-tune GPT-3 using supervised learning.

sentence_reviews.text: Supervised fine-tuning & instruction tuning uses Large-scale pretraining as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Ouyang arXiv abstract line 38 starts from GPT-3 and fine-tunes it; T5 arXiv abstract line 38 describes pre-training before fine-tuning on downstream tasks.
sentence_reviews.notes: Directly supported for instruction-tuned LLM pipelines.

sentence_reviews.text: Supervised fine-tuning & instruction tuning uses Labels, targets, rewards & preferences as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Ouyang arXiv abstract lines 38-39 describes labeler demonstrations, rankings of model outputs, supervised fine-tuning, and human evaluations.
sentence_reviews.notes: Labels/targets are directly supported for SFT; rewards/preferences are more tied to the subsequent RLHF stage.

edge_reviews.notes: Supervised learning is explicitly used for instruction fine-tuning; core strength is supported.
Fine-tuning pretrained language models is directly supported; core strength is appropriate for this node.
Demonstrations and rankings support supervision signals, but the combined labels/targets/rewards/preferences label is broader than SFT alone.
